title: Install Z-Axis Sensor And Route CLINK Wiring
summary: Mount the Z-axis sensor on the rear side, route the Z-axis wire down through the column and out the lower grommet into the control box, connect the wire to a documented control box input, and restart the operator station using the referenced startup procedure.
when_to_use: Use when performing the documented Z-axis sensor installation and CLINK wiring connection described in the OptiSweep Operation and Maintenance Manual.
not_for: Do not use for selecting among I/O1, DI6, or DI7 when the source does not provide criteria for that choice.
Do not use when the referenced operator station restart procedure on page 66 is unavailable.

responsibilities: Route the Z-axis wiring through the documented path
Connect the wire to the documented control box input
Restart the operator station using the referenced startup procedure

safety_notes: This procedure is not marked support-safe in the candidate.
Do not invent connection selection logic for I/O1, DI6, or DI7 beyond what is documented in the source.
access_or_tools_needed: Physical access to the sensor mounting location
Access to the rear-side wire routing hole
Access to the column, lower grommet, and control box
Referenced procedure: Starting The Operator Station

access_required: Physical access to the sensor mounting location
Access to the rear-side wire routing hole
Access to the column, lower grommet, and control box
system_preconditions: Z-axis sensor and wiring are available for installation
The referenced startup procedure for the operator station is available
external_references: Starting The Operator Station procedure on page 66

visual_references.description: Figure 7-2 showing Z-axis CLINK wire routing and documented control box connection points.

steps.title: Mount the Z-axis sensor at the rear-side routing hole
steps.purpose: Position and secure the Z-axis sensor at the documented mounting location.
steps.instruction: Feed the sensor through the wire routing hole on the rear side and fasten it in place using the M3 button-head screw.
steps.expected_result: The Z-axis sensor is installed at the rear-side mounting location and secured with the M3 button-head screw.
steps.healthy_condition: The sensor is fed through the rear wire routing hole and is fastened in place.
steps.failure_condition: The rear wire routing hole or mounting location cannot be identified, or the sensor cannot be secured with the documented screw.

actions.label: Feed sensor through rear routing hole
actions.exact_action: Feed the sensor through the wire routing hole on the rear side.

actions.label: Fasten sensor
actions.exact_action: Fasten it in place using the M3 button-head screw.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Rear-side wire routing hole and Z-axis sensor mounting location referenced by the installation text.

stop_or_escalate_if: The installer cannot identify the rear wire routing hole from the available source material.
The installer cannot identify the sensor mounting location from the available source material.

source_evidence.text: Feed the sensor through the wire routing hole on the rear side and fasten it in place using the M3 button-head screw.
The associated installation instructions say to feed the sensor through the rear wire routing hole and fasten it using an M3 button-head screw.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-2 appears on page 107 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_2_1_2_installation.

steps.title: Route the Z-axis wire through the column to the control box
steps.purpose: Place the Z-axis wire along the documented path from the sensor to the control box.
steps.instruction: Route the Z-axis wire down through the column and out the lower grommet into the control box.
steps.expected_result: The Z-axis wire follows the documented path through the column and exits the lower grommet into the control box.
steps.healthy_condition: The wire is routed down through the column and out the lower grommet into the control box as shown in the source.
steps.failure_condition: The wire path through the column or lower grommet cannot be identified or completed using the available source material.

actions.label: Route wire through column
actions.exact_action: Route the Z-axis wire down through the column.

actions.label: Route wire through lower grommet
actions.exact_action: Route the wire out the lower grommet into the control box.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Z-axis wire routing path through the column and lower grommet exit into the control box.

stop_or_escalate_if: The installer cannot identify the lower grommet from the available source material.
The documented wire routing path through the column is unclear or cannot be followed.

source_evidence.text: Route the Z-axis wire down through the column and out the lower grommet into the control box.
The nearby text states that Z-axis wires route down through the column and out the lower grommet into the control box.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-2 appears on page 107 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_2_1_2_installation.

steps.title: Connect the Z-axis wire in the control box
steps.purpose: Terminate the routed Z-axis wire at one of the documented control box inputs.
steps.instruction: Plug the Z-axis wire into either I/O1, DI6, or DI7 in the control box.
steps.expected_result: The Z-axis wire is connected to a documented control box input.
steps.healthy_condition: The wire is plugged into one of the documented locations: I/O1, DI6, or DI7.
steps.failure_condition: The control box connection points cannot be identified, or the installer cannot determine how to proceed because the source does not explain how to choose among I/O1, DI6, or DI7.

actions.label: Connect Z-axis wire
actions.exact_action: Plug the Z-axis wire into either I/O1, DI6, or DI7 in the control box.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Documented control box connection points labeled I/O1, DI6, or DI7.

stop_or_escalate_if: The installer cannot identify the documented I/O1, DI6, or DI7 locations from the available source material.
The source does not provide criteria for choosing among I/O1, DI6, or DI7.

source_evidence.text: Plug the Z-axis wire into either I/O1, DI6, or DI7 in the control box.
The nearby text states that the Z-axis wires plug into either I/O1, DI6, or DI7.
The source states the wire plugs into either I/O1, DI6, or DI7 but does not explain how to choose among them; that ambiguity should be reviewed before final runbook creation.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-2 appears on page 107 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_2_1_2_installation.

steps.title: Restart the operator station
steps.purpose: Return the operator station to service using the referenced startup procedure.
steps.instruction: Re-start the operator station using the referenced startup procedure on page 66.
steps.expected_result: The operator station is restarted using the referenced procedure.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator station restart is completed using the documented startup reference.
steps.failure_condition: The referenced operator station restart procedure is not available.

actions.label: Restart operator station
actions.exact_action: Re-start the operator station using the referenced startup procedure on page 66.

stop_or_escalate_if: The referenced operator station restart procedure is not available.

source_evidence.text: Re-start the operator station using the referenced startup procedure on page 66.
The restart step references another procedure on page 66 rather than restating it here.

success_criteria: The Z-axis sensor is mounted at the documented rear-side location.
The Z-axis wire is routed down through the column and out the lower grommet into the control box.
The wire is connected to a documented control box input.
The operator station is restarted using the referenced startup procedure.
healthy_conditions: Sensor is secured with the M3 button-head screw.
Wire routing matches the documented path shown for the Z-axis CLINK connections.
Connection is made at one of the documented inputs: I/O1, DI6, or DI7.
Operator station restart procedure is available and completed.
failure_conditions: Rear wire routing hole or mounting location cannot be identified.
Lower grommet or wire routing path through the column cannot be identified.
Control box connection points I/O1, DI6, or DI7 cannot be identified.
The source does not provide criteria for choosing among I/O1, DI6, or DI7.
The referenced operator station restart procedure is not available.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the installer cannot identify the rear wire routing hole, lower grommet, control box connection points, or the documented I/O1, DI6, or DI7 locations from the available source material.
Escalate if the referenced operator station restart procedure is not available.
Escalate for SME review if a selection must be made among I/O1, DI6, or DI7 because the source does not explain how to choose.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall Z-axis CLINK connection diagram, including routing path and documented connection points.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-2 appears on page 107 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_2_1_2_installation.

missing_details: The source packet does not include the actual body text of section 7.3.2.1.2; step wording is grounded in the candidate and artifact retrieval text.
The source does not explain how to choose among I/O1, DI6, or DI7.
The referenced operator station startup procedure on page 66 is not included in this packet.
No explicit production stop, LOTO requirement, or time estimate is provided in the supplied packet for this procedure.
